differentials.name: Interbronchial diverticulum =  traction diverticulum =  response to pull from fibrous adhesions following lymph node infection (TB), contains all 3 esophageal layers
differentials.raw: Interbronchial diverticulum =  traction diverticulum =  response to pull from fibrous adhesions following lymph node infection (TB), contains all 3 esophageal layers

differentials.name: Interaorticobronchial diverticulum =  thoracic pulsion diverticulum Location:   on left anterolateral wall between inferior border of aortic arch + upper margin of left main bronchus
differentials.raw: Interaorticobronchial diverticulum =  thoracic pulsion diverticulum Location:   on left anterolateral wall between inferior border of aortic arch + upper margin of left main bronchus

context: Location:   usually on right anterolateral wall of interbronchial segment √  calcified mediastinal nodes Location:   usually on lateral esophageal wall, right > left, in distal 10 cm √  often associated with hiatus hernia Cx:   overflow aspiration √  outpouching from mucosal glands Tracheobronchoesophageal Fistula =  ESOPHAGORESPIRATORY FISTULA Location: esophagotracheal 52-57% esophagobronchial 37-40% esophagopulmonary 3-11% CXR: √  recurrent pulmonary consolidation √  lung abscess √  pleural effusion Esophagography: ◊   Use low-osmolar hydrosoluble contrast material √   may confirm Dx + iden
